artifact.title: Create a change order

story.goal: Document a scope change on a project with a cost impact so it can be sent to the client for in-app approval.
steps: The project manager selects a project and opens the change order creation form.
The project manager enters a title, a detailed scope description, and the cost amount.
The system validates that the project exists and creates the change order in draft status with a generated id and timestamps.
The change order is saved and ready to be sent to the client for in-app approval.
story.outcome: A new change order record exists in draft status, linked to the selected project, with title, scope description, and cost amount captured.

inputs.description: Short title summarizing the scope change.
Detailed description of the scope change being requested.
Cost impact of the change order in USD.
The project this change order belongs to, resolved from the currently selected project.
System-generated unique identifier for the new change order.
Initial status set to draft upon creation.
Timestamp when the change order record is created.
Timestamp of the last modification, set to creation time initially.

contextResolution.description: The backend resolves projectId from the currently selected project in the workspace, validating that the project exists before creating the change order.
The backend generates a new UUID for the change order record at creation time.
The backend sets the initial status to 'draft' as the default for newly created change orders.
The backend sets createdAt to the current server timestamp at creation time.
The backend sets updatedAt to the current server timestamp at creation time.

acceptanceAssertions: After creation the change order exists with status 'draft'.
The change order is linked to a valid project via projectId.
The change order has a non-empty title, scopeDescription, and a positive amount.
The change order does not affect job costing or invoicing because its status is 'draft', not 'approved'.
No e-signature is required for the change order; it uses in-app approval status workflow.
A standalone change order without a project association cannot be created.

errors.message: operation createChangeOrder: systemDefault originRef "systemDefault" must be one of systemDefault.now, systemDefault.uuid, systemDefault.locale
